title: 4 charged in connection with alleged kidnapping, beating near Nantucket park
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Four Nantucket men are charged with kidnapping and assaulting a 28-year-old man near Winter Park.
Suspects allegedly used a wooden stake and an extension cord during the attack, causing non-life-threatening injuries.
Calray Brown, Dijon Hazel, Newton Lawrence, and Alvin Barnett face multiple charges and are set for arraignment on Monday.

title: 2 arrested at Logan Airport as ICE activity ramps up
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Maria Rosales and David Ardila were arrested by ICE at Logan Airport amid ramped-up activity.
Ardila and Rosales, detained in Massachusetts, face immigration proceedings while awaiting asylum and DACA status updates.
Their attorney reports increased ICE arrests at Logan, highlighting legal disputes over their detention amid pending applications.

title: 5 movies to watch after ‘The Odyssey’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Interstellar' (2014) offers thematic parallels to 'The Odyssey' with a time-bending family quest. Available on Hulu, Paramount+.
'Lawrence of Arabia' (1962) and 'The Searchers' (1956) are epic films with narrative connections to Homer's story.
'O Brother, Where Art Thou?' (2000) and 'The SpongeBob SquarePants Movie' (2004) adapt 'The Odyssey' with humor and animation styles.

title: Formidable Red Sox extend win streak to 11 games with doubleheader sweep of Rays
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Boston Red Sox extended their winning streak to 11 games by sweeping the Tampa Bay Rays in a doubleheader.
Boston's Game 1 10-0 win featured strong pitching from Jake Bennett and a six-run sixth inning.
The Red Sox improved to .500 for the first time since March, securing the third wild-card spot in the AL.

title: Chad Tracy offers updates on several injured Red Sox pitchers
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Ranger Suarez, on the injured list with a left groin strain, might start for the Red Sox on July 21.
Connelly Early, recovering from left elbow inflammation, resumed throwing but has no set date for return to play.
Kutter Crawford, Tanner Houck, and Johan Oviedo are progressing well in rehab, with Houck now throwing off a mound twice weekly.

title: 4 Red Sox who rose their stock following Friday’s doubleheader sweep vs. Rays
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Masataka Yoshida hit a home run and contributed multiple hits, enhancing his role as Boston's primary designated hitter.
Caleb Durbin excelled with five hits in two games, increasing his effectiveness despite no home runs against the Rays.
Jake Bennett pitched six scoreless innings, aiding bullpen management, while Carlos Narvaez hit a home run and three RBIs.

title: Robert Kraft backs Mike Vrabel after controversial offseason: ‘No one is infallible’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots owner Robert Kraft supports head coach Mike Vrabel, stating 'no one is infallible' on ESPN's First Take.
Mike Vrabel faced scrutiny after being photographed with reporter Dianna Russini, who later resigned from The Athletic.
Patriots training camp is set to begin next weekend in Foxborough as they prepare for the upcoming NFL season.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Anya Taylor-Joy stars in 'Lucky', a seven-episode series now streaming on Apple TV.
'The Hunt for the Wilderpeople', featuring Sam Neill, is available on Prime Video and Plex.
Curry Barker's horror film 'Obsession' grossed over $400 million; it's streaming on Peacock.

title: NH judge: Concord man violated Civil Rights Act in assault on transgender woman
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Travis Lufkin of Concord violated NH Civil Rights Act by assaulting a transgender woman in 2024, per court ruling.
Lufkin called the victim a slur during the attack at a Speedway store; court cites bias based on gender identity.
Lufkin faces a $5,000 civil fine and contact restrictions; received suspended jail sentences for assault charges.

title: Red Sox’ trade deadline plans ‘fluid,’ ESPN’s Jeff Passan says
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox' trade deadline strategy remains 'fluid,' per ESPN's Jeff Passan, with options considered before Aug. 3.
Boston's recent 10-game winning streak boosts playoff odds, yet decisions on Aroldis Chapman and others remain uncertain.
The Red Sox may seek a right-handed hitter, with aggressive interest noted in past reports, as the deadline approaches.
